name: Angel of the Winds Casino
notes: Tulalip-area casino. Concerts, dining, hotel.

name: Edmonds Center for the Arts
notes: Performing arts venue. Music, theater, comedy, community events.

name: Salish Sea Brewing
notes: Craft brewery in downtown Edmonds. Near ferry terminal.

name: Edmonds Fishing Pier
notes: Year-round fishing. Squid jigging at night in summer. Adjacent to underwater dive park.

name: Boeing Everett Factory
notes: Worlds largest building by volume. 787, 777X, 767 assembly. Public tours.

name: Flying Heritage & Combat Armor Museum
notes: Paul Allen collection. WWII aircraft and tanks. Weekend flights of vintage aircraft.

name: Future of Flight
notes: Boeing tour departure. Aviation exhibits, rooftop viewing platform.

name: Imagine Children's Museum
notes: Interactive children's museum. 3 floors of hands-on exhibits.

name: Museum of Flight Restoration Center
notes: Watch aircraft restoration in progress. Free admission.

name: Everett Civic Auditorium
notes: Concert and event venue. Capacity ~1,700.

name: Schack Art Center
notes: Community art center. Glassblowing, galleries, workshops.

name: Village Theatre
notes: Professional musical theatre. Issaquah + Everett campuses.

name: Xfinity Arena (Angel of the Winds Arena)
notes: Everett Silvertips (WHL hockey). Concerts. Capacity ~8,100.

name: Casino Road food corridor
notes: Dozens of restaurants: Vietnamese pho, Korean BBQ, Mexican taquerias, Somali, Filipino, Indian. Best affordable diverse food in south Everett.

name: Scuttlebutt Brewing
notes: Waterfront brewpub at the marina. Local craft beer since 1996.

name: Jetty Island
notes: Free summer ferry (Jul-Sep). 2-mile sandy beach, warm shallow water, bird watching.

name: Port of Everett Marina
notes: 13-lane boat launch ($15/day). Direct access to Possession Sound. Crabbing, fishing, shrimping.

name: Funko HQ
notes: Funko headquarters. Store with exclusives, history, photo ops.

name: Paine Field Terminal
notes: Alaska Airlines commercial flights. Opened 2019. Boutique terminal.

name: Alderwood Mall
notes: Major regional shopping center. Dining, retail, entertainment.

name: H Mart
notes: Korean grocery. Produce, seafood, prepared foods, food court.

name: Lynnwood City Center Station
notes: Sound Transit 1 Line and 2 Line terminus. Bus connections to Mukilteo, Everett, Edmonds.

name: Diamond Knot Brewing
notes: Local craft brewery. Burgers, fish & chips, house beers. Established 1994.

name: Ivars Mukilteo Landing
notes: Waterfront seafood. Clam chowder, fish & chips. Views of ferry traffic and Whidbey Island.

name: Red Cup Cafe
notes: Breakfast and brunch. Popular weekends. Local favorite.

name: Whidbey Coffee
notes: Drive-through and walk-in espresso. Local roaster from Whidbey Island.

name: Harbour Pointe Golf Club
notes: 18-hole championship course. Restaurant. South Mukilteo.

name: Japanese Gulch
notes: 160-acre urban forest. Historic Japanese logging community site. Trail network.

name: Mukilteo Lighthouse
notes: Historic 1906 lighthouse. Beach, boat launch, picnic area. Free admission.

name: KEXP
notes: Independent music station. Live sessions, DJ shows. The heart of PNW music. 90.3 FM.

name: KNKX
notes: NPR + jazz/blues. Puget Sound public radio. Formerly KPLU. 88.5 FM.

name: Tulalip Resort Casino
notes: Full resort. Concert venue, spa, dining. Seattle Premium Outlets adjacent.
